references.title: University student retention: Best time and data to identify undergraduate students at risk of dropout
references.journal: Innovations in Education and Teaching International

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Being at Risk of Dropping out of School
extracted.Courses: 1 Electromechanical Engineering Degree over 4 Academic Years
extracted.Features: [Gender, InterItinerary, AccessUniv, SubAccessUniv, EorkExpPrevYear, MarkUnivAccess, PassInterChemistry, PassInterPhysiscs, PassInterCalculusI, PassInterGE, PaassInterIT, AsInterAlg_Geom, PassPhysics, PassCalculusI, PassIT, PasGE, *assIT_GE]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The create 3 models, one per moment of prediction:

1 - Induction Week

2 - Semester Halfway Point - Variables PassInter are added

3- End of First Semester - Variables Pass are added.
extracted.Comments: Authors explore 3 distinct moments of prediction dduring the first semester to identify Dropout.
extracted.Limitations:
No Separate Validation or Test Dataset → Results published refer to training Data Only.

Small Sample Size

Evaluates Model Performance using Accuracy.

annotations.text: There don’t seem be many studies actually looking into identifying the optimal time to identify dropout. Authors use demographics and academic data from 935 students from an engineering school and use DT predict dropout (withdrawal). To identify the optimal time, authors choose 3 distinct moments of prediction during the first-year’s first semester. Authors find academic is a good early predictor of dropout.
